mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightness and wage-driven demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and real personal savings indicates synchronized supply recovery and consumer spending power, creating a favorable earnings environment that lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change × RSAFS change) shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression predicting 20-day forward SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth signals late-cycle demand exhaustion, which compresses forward earnings multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where GDPC1 growth decelerates for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate tightens financial conditions and raises borrowing costs, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in employment levels and decline in the unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over a 60-day horizon in more than 40% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO rises for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts signals rising household wealth and construction sector momentum, which boosts related corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The trigger produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances across a 10-year backtest.
